title: Regression in podman 4.3.0-dev connection on windows (ssh handshake fails)
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

Podman remote on windows stopped working w/o modifying known_hosts file on Windows platform somewhere in 4.3.0-dev. 4.2.0 works as expected

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Install latest podman 4.2.0 msi

2. create default WSL machine and start it

3. Build locally podman-remote 2 times - one from v4.2.0 tag and one from main HEAD.

4. Remove podman binary from installer (or rename to .bak)

5. Run `podman ps` for both self compiled binaries

```bat
C:\Users\User>podman-4.2.0 ps
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE       COMMAND     CREATED     STATUS      PORTS       NAMES

C:\Users\User>podman-4.3.0-dev ps
Error: failed to connect: ssh: handshake failed: knownhosts: key is unknown
```

**Describe the results you received:**

```
Error: failed to connect: ssh: handshake failed: knownhosts: key is unknown
```

For binary compiled from main HEAD

**Describe the results you expected:**

The same as for one built from tag
```
CONTAINER ID  IMAGE       COMMAND     CREATED     STATUS      PORTS       NAMES
```

Discovered as part of this experiment https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/13006 but is reproducible for the unmodified podman installation with default feature set.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

The workaround is to manually add records to trusted hosts. In details described here https://github.com/arixmkii/qcw#2-machine-keys-are-not-added-to-known_hosts-by-default

**Output of `podman version`:**

4.2.0
```
C:\Users\User>podman-4.2.0 version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.2.0
API Version:  4.2.0
Go Version:   go1.18
Git Commit:   7fe5a419cfd2880df2028ad3d7fd9378a88a04f4
Built:        Thu Sep  1 13:57:12 2022
OS/Arch:      windows/amd64

Server:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.2.0
API Version:  4.2.0
Go Version:   go1.18.4
Built:        Thu Aug 11 17:42:17 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

4.3.0-dev version output  fails due to ssh as well.

**Output of `podman info`:**

```
(paste your output here)
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
(paste your output here)
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes. Both versions built from sources.

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Windows WSL2

comments.body: Please remove "feature" label. Didn't notice it, while creating issue on my test laptop (to get command output added to the description).

Thanks for the report, sounds like this is likely  related to the new ssh work here:
https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/15094

FYI @baude @ashley-cui @jwhonce. The new stricter approach in #15094 with host key validation probably breaks podman machine (mac, windows, etc) since its normal for the machine identity to change as people machine rm/machine init. Not sure if you guys looked at this yet, but I am guessing we should update client server known host keys as part of machine init

@n1hility I have a few PRs in flight to fix the key validation:

containers/common#1136 and #15471. The changes breaking this did not make it into 4.2 so this will be fixed by next major release.
Was experiencing this as recently as 4.3.0-rc1. Can confirm that upgrading to 4.3.0 fixed this for me on MacOS. Thanks for the fix!
